userProfile: single student, low budget, small kitchen

recipes.title: Tomato Egg Rice Bowl

steps.text: Cook the rice in the rice cooker.
Warm the tomatoes and spinach in a pan.
Whisk in the eggs and stir gently until softly set.
Spoon over the rice and eat warm.

substitutions: Use frozen peas instead of spinach.
Use couscous instead of rice.

recipes.title: Red Lentil Tomato Soup

steps.text: Soften the onion in a little oil.
Add lentils, tomatoes, the broth cube, and 500 ml water.
Simmer until the lentils are very soft.
Whisk or mash lightly for a smoother texture if wanted.

substitutions: Use carrot instead of onion.
Add a spoon of yogurt at the end.

recipes.title: Yogurt Banana Oats Bowl

steps.text: Put the yogurt in a bowl.
Slice the banana on top.
Add oats and cinnamon. This counts as a meal.

substitutions: Use fromage blanc instead of yogurt.
Use apple or pear instead of banana.

recipes.title: Tuna Sweetcorn Rice Bowl

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Drain the tuna and sweetcorn.
Slice the cucumber and stir yogurt with a little salt.
Build the bowl and eat warm or room temperature.

substitutions: Use canned peas instead of sweetcorn.
Use couscous instead of rice.

recipes.title: Chickpea Couscous Bowl

steps.text: Cover the couscous with hot kettle water and a plate.
Drain the chickpeas and grate or finely chop the carrot.
Fluff the couscous and mix everything together with oil and lemon juice.

substitutions: Use raisins for a slightly sweeter version.
Use canned lentils instead of chickpeas.

recipes.title: Sardine Tomato Pasta

steps.text: Boil the pasta.
Warm garlic, tomatoes, and sardines in a pan, breaking the fish gently.
Toss with the pasta and serve.

substitutions: Use tuna instead of sardines.
Add spinach if you want a vegetable boost.

recipes.title: Spinach Egg Pasta

steps.text: Cook the pasta.
Warm the spinach in a pan.
Add the drained pasta and whisked eggs; stir off the heat until glossy and just set.

substitutions: Use peas instead of spinach.
Add grated cheese if that feels more comfortable.

recipes.title: White Bean Garlic Soup

steps.text: Cook onion and garlic in oil until soft.
Add beans, broth cube, and 400 ml water.
Simmer gently.
Mash some of the beans with a whisk for body and eat warm.

substitutions: Use canned lentils instead of white beans.
Add spinach at the end.

recipes.title: Carrot Lentil Rice Pot

steps.text: Dice or grate the carrot.
Put rice, lentils, carrot, broth cube, and 500 ml water in a pot.
Simmer until soft, stirring once or twice.

substitutions: Use couscous if you need a quicker version.
Add yogurt on top to soften the flavor.

recipes.title: Peanut Soy Noodles

steps.text: Cook the noodles.
Stir peanut butter and soy sauce with a splash of hot water until smooth.
Add peas for the last 2 minutes, then drain and coat the noodles.

substitutions: Use pasta if noodles are cheaper.
Use sunflower seed butter if needed.

recipes.title: Fromage Blanc Apple Oats Bowl

steps.text: Put the fromage blanc in a bowl.
Add grated or finely chopped apple.
Scatter oats on top. This is enough for a meal.

substitutions: Use yogurt instead of fromage blanc.
Use pear instead of apple.

recipes.title: Rice Cooker Pea Egg Rice

steps.text: Cook the rice in the rice cooker.
When nearly done, stir in peas and crack in the eggs.
Close the lid until the eggs set softly.
Season with soy sauce.

substitutions: Add spinach instead of peas.
Use a pan if the rice cooker is busy.

recipes.title: Courgette Tomato Couscous

steps.text: Cover the couscous with hot water and let it stand.
Cook chopped courgette in a pan with oil until soft.
Add tomatoes, then fold through the couscous.

substitutions: Use carrot instead of courgette.
Add chickpeas for more protein.

recipes.title: Tomato Chickpea Pasta

steps.text: Boil the pasta.
Warm chickpeas and tomatoes in a pan.
Toss together and season lightly.

substitutions: Use white beans instead of chickpeas.
Add spinach if desired.

recipes.title: Mushroom Pea Rice

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Cook sliced mushrooms in a pan until soft.
Add peas for the last 2 minutes and serve over rice.

substitutions: Use courgette instead of mushrooms.
Add an egg on top for more protein.

recipes.title: Tuna White Bean Salad

steps.text: Drain the tuna and beans.
Chop the tomato and cucumber.
Mix everything together with a little oil and salt.

substitutions: Use chickpeas instead of white beans.
Use sardines instead of tuna.

recipes.title: Lentil Dijon Potato Salad

steps.text: Boil the potatoes until tender.
Drain and slice them.
Mix mustard with yogurt and stir through the potatoes, lentils, and a little onion.

substitutions: Use white beans if lentils are unavailable.
Skip the onion for a softer flavor.

recipes.title: Simple Apple Porridge

steps.text: Cook oats and milk in a small pot, stirring often.
Grate or chop the apple.
Add apple and cinnamon and warm through briefly.

substitutions: Use water plus yogurt if milk is not on hand.
Use pear or banana instead of apple.

recipes.title: Leek White Bean Pasta

steps.text: Cook the pasta.
Cook sliced leek in oil until very soft.
Add beans and a spoon of pasta water, then stir in yogurt off the heat and toss with pasta.

substitutions: Use onion instead of leek.
Use chickpeas instead of white beans.

recipes.title: Rice with Peas and Scrambled Egg

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Warm the peas in a pan.
Scramble the eggs gently with a little butter.
Serve the eggs and peas over the rice.

substitutions: Use spinach instead of peas.
Use couscous if you need it faster.

recipes.title: Sardine Potato Salad

steps.text: Boil the potatoes until tender.
Drain and cool slightly.
Mix with sardines, yogurt, lemon juice, and cucumber.

substitutions: Use tuna instead of sardines.
Use canned lentils if you do not want potatoes.

recipes.title: Tomato Olive Pasta

steps.text: Boil the pasta.
Warm tomatoes and olives in a pan.
Toss together and eat.

substitutions: Add tuna for more protein.
Use capers instead of olives.

recipes.title: Egg Spinach Couscous Bowl

steps.text: Cover the couscous with hot water.
Warm the spinach in a pan.
Scramble the eggs gently and serve everything together.

substitutions: Use peas instead of spinach.
Use rice instead of couscous.

recipes.title: White Bean Tomato Skillet

steps.text: Cook the garlic in a little oil.
Add tomatoes and beans and simmer gently.
Mash a few beans if you want it thicker.

substitutions: Use chickpeas instead of white beans.
Add spinach at the end.

recipes.title: Carrot Cumin Lentil Soup

steps.text: Cook the chopped carrots in oil with cumin.
Add lentils, broth cube, and 500 ml water.
Simmer until soft.
Whisk lightly if you want it smoother.

substitutions: Use curry powder instead of cumin.
Add yogurt on top.

recipes.title: Yogurt Chickpea Cucumber Bowl

steps.text: If you want, soak the couscous with a little hot kettle water.
Drain the chickpeas and chop the cucumber.
Stir everything together with a little salt and eat cold or room temperature.

substitutions: Use white beans instead of chickpeas.
Use rice left from another day instead of couscous.

recipes.title: Tomato Tuna Couscous Pot

steps.text: Cover the couscous with hot water.
Drain the tuna.
Warm the tomatoes if you want, then mix with the couscous and tuna.

substitutions: Use sardines instead of tuna.
Add sweetcorn if that feels familiar.

recipes.title: Creamy Pea Pasta

steps.text: Cook the pasta.
Add peas for the last 2 minutes.
Drain, then stir in yogurt and pepper off the heat.

substitutions: Use fromage blanc instead of yogurt.
Add grated cheese if wanted.

recipes.title: Lentil Carrot Bowl

steps.text: Cover the couscous with hot water.
Grate or finely chop the carrot.
Drain the lentils and mix everything together with oil and lemon juice.

substitutions: Use chickpeas instead of lentils.
Use rice instead of couscous.

recipes.title: Garlic Spinach Rice with Egg

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Cook garlic and spinach in a pan.
Scramble the eggs softly in the same pan.
Serve over the rice.

substitutions: Use peas instead of spinach.
Use couscous if you need less time.

recipes.title: Chickpea Spinach Curry Rice

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Warm tomatoes with curry powder in a pan.
Add chickpeas and spinach and simmer until hot.
Serve over the rice.

substitutions: Use lentils instead of chickpeas.
Stir in yogurt if you want a softer flavor.

recipes.title: Tofu Broccoli Rice Bowl

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Cook tofu cubes in a little oil until warmed and lightly colored.
Add broccoli and soy sauce and cook until tender.
Serve over the rice.

substitutions: Use egg instead of tofu.
Use peas instead of broccoli.

recipes.title: Harissa Egg Tomato Skillet

steps.text: Warm the tomatoes with a little harissa.
Crack in the eggs and cover the pan until set to your liking.
Eat with bread or over rice.

substitutions: Use paprika instead of harissa for a softer version.
Use couscous instead of bread.

recipes.title: Couscous with Chickpeas and Raisins

steps.text: Cover the couscous with hot water.
Drain the chickpeas.
Fluff the couscous and mix in chickpeas, raisins, cumin, and oil.

substitutions: Use lentils instead of chickpeas.
Skip raisins if sweet notes are not comfortable.

recipes.title: Mackerel Lemon Potato Bowl

steps.text: Boil the potatoes until tender.
Drain and crush lightly in a bowl.
Top with mackerel, yogurt, and lemon juice.

substitutions: Use sardines instead of mackerel.
Add cucumber for freshness.

recipes.title: Peanut Chickpea Noodles

steps.text: Cook the noodles.
Stir peanut butter and soy sauce with hot water.
Drain chickpeas, grate the carrot, and toss everything together.

substitutions: Use pasta if noodles are not available.
Use sunflower seed butter if needed.

recipes.title: Tomato Lentil Coconut Rice

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Simmer lentils, tomatoes, coconut milk, curry powder, and 200 ml water.
Serve the lentils over the rice.

substitutions: Use yogurt instead of coconut milk for a cheaper option.
Use chickpeas instead of red lentils.

recipes.title: Tomato Courgette White Bean Pasta

steps.text: Cook the pasta.
Cook chopped courgette in oil until soft.
Add tomatoes and beans, simmer briefly, then toss with pasta.

substitutions: Use carrot instead of courgette.
Use chickpeas instead of white beans.

recipes.title: Mustard Lentil Warm Salad

steps.text: Boil the potatoes until tender.
Drain the lentils and grate the carrot.
Mix mustard and oil, then toss everything together while the potatoes are still warm.

substitutions: Use yogurt in the dressing for a milder result.
Use white beans instead of lentils.

recipes.title: Red Lentil Dhal-Style Soup

steps.text: Cook onion in oil with curry powder.
Add lentils, coconut milk, and 400 ml water.
Simmer until the lentils are soft.
Whisk briefly for a smoother texture if wanted.

substitutions: Use yogurt instead of coconut milk.
Serve over rice to make it more substantial.

recipes.title: Sardine Lemon Pasta with Spinach

steps.text: Cook the pasta.
Warm sardines and spinach gently in a pan.
Add lemon juice, toss with the pasta, and serve.

substitutions: Use tuna instead of sardines.
Use peas instead of spinach.

recipes.title: Courgette Chickpea Tomato Rice

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Cook courgette in oil until soft.
Add tomatoes and chickpeas and simmer briefly.
Serve with the rice.

substitutions: Use frozen spinach instead of courgette.
Use lentils instead of chickpeas.

recipes.title: Peanut Tofu Couscous Bowl

steps.text: Cover the couscous with hot water.
Warm tofu in a pan.
Stir peanut butter and soy sauce with a little hot water and pour over the bowl with grated carrot.

substitutions: Use chickpeas instead of tofu.
Use sunflower seed butter if needed.

recipes.title: Tomato Bean Rice Skillet

steps.text: Cook the rice.
Warm beans, tomatoes, and sweetcorn in a pan.
Serve over the rice.

substitutions: Use chickpeas instead of white beans.
Add a fried egg on top if wanted.

recipes.title: Warm Potato Egg Spinach Salad

steps.text: Boil the potatoes until tender.
Hard-boil or soft-boil the eggs in the same pot.
Warm the spinach briefly, then mix everything with mustard and yogurt.

substitutions: Use peas instead of spinach.
Use canned lentils if potatoes feel too heavy.

recipes.title: Rice Cooker Tomato Lentils

steps.text: Put lentils, tomatoes, broth cube, oil, and 500 ml water in the rice cooker.
Cook until very soft.
Whisk lightly and eat as a thick soup or over rice.

substitutions: Stir in yogurt after cooking for a milder taste.
Add spinach in the last few minutes.

recipes.title: Semolina Yogurt Fruit Bowl

steps.text: Wet the couscous with a little hot water so it softens quickly.
Add yogurt and sliced banana.
Drizzle with honey if wanted. This is enough for now.

substitutions: Use oats instead of couscous.
Use fromage blanc instead of yogurt.

recipes.title: Hummus Chickpea Cucumber Plate

steps.text: Put hummus on a plate or in a bowl.
Add drained chickpeas and sliced cucumber.
Eat with bread. Packaged foods are allowed here.

substitutions: Use crackers instead of bread.
Use yogurt instead of hummus if needed.

recipes.title: Warm White Bean Tomato Couscous

steps.text: Cover the couscous with hot water.
Warm beans and tomatoes in a pan.
Spoon over the couscous and eat warm.

substitutions: Use chickpeas instead of white beans.
Add spinach if you want more vegetables.

recipes.title: Carrot Egg Fried Rice-Style Bowl

steps.text: Cook the rice and let it cool a little if you have time.
Cook grated carrot and peas in a pan.
Add rice and whisked eggs and stir until the eggs set.
Season with soy sauce.

substitutions: Use couscous for a softer, quicker version.
Use spinach instead of peas.
